description: Detects expensive EC2 GPU, Inferentia, or Trainium instances and fleets that run during idle windows without start/stop scheduling or autoscaling controls.

severity_rules.name: unscheduled_accelerator_off_hours
severity_rules.condition: accelerator_instance == true AND off_hours_per_week >= 40 AND no_schedule_control == true
severity_rules.description: Accelerator instances run during likely idle windows without schedule automation. Add Instance Scheduler, SSM Quick Setup, EventBridge, or ASG scheduled actions.

severity_rules.name: idle_dev_training_gpu
severity_rules.condition: accelerator_instance == true AND workload_role in [dev, training, notebook] AND gpu_utilization_avg_pct < 20 AND no_schedule_control == true
severity_rules.description: Development or training accelerator has low utilization and should be stopped automatically outside approved windows after checkpoint safety review.

severity_rules.name: fixed_gpu_asg_capacity
severity_rules.condition: gpu_launch_template_or_asg == true AND desired_capacity_static == true AND no_scheduled_action_or_scaling == true
severity_rules.description: GPU Auto Scaling group keeps fixed desired capacity. Add scaling or scheduled actions if the workload has predictable idle windows.

severity_rules.name: residual_costs_not_modeled
severity_rules.description: Stopping EC2 instances does not remove EBS, snapshot, Elastic IP, or some network costs. Model residual costs before presenting net savings.

validation_before_change: Check workload role, SLA, checkpointing, and warm-up time.
Verify ASG scheduled actions do not conflict with target tracking policies.
Model residual EBS, EIP, snapshot, NAT, and data transfer costs.
